figures.caption: Figure 3: Cost sensitivity analysis on DTLZ2 (𝑚 = 3). QUIVER gradually reduces IA usage as the cost ratio increases, demonstrating cost-aware modality selection.
Figure 2: Budget allocation for QUIVER across benchmarks. Evaluations dominate the cost budget (∼92%), with IA fraction among preference queries ranging from 20% (easy DTLZ2) to 35% (hard WFG9).

Table 2: Action counts for QUIVER (mean over 5 seeds, 𝐵 = 500). Shows the number of PS queries (n_ps), IA queries (n_ia), and IA fraction among preference queries. The policy adapts modality selection to problem difficulty.
Figure 1: Utility regret comparison across benchmarks. Preference-learning methods achieve near-zero regret while Eval-only has 23–34% regret.
